description: A Chinese martial system spanning hundreds of styles. Trains circular power, fluid footwork, and deceptive strikes through stances, forms, and application drills.

movements.name: Zhi Quan (Straight Punch)

commonMistakes.description: Raising the punching shoulder and tensing the trapezius throughout the strike, which slows arm extension and reduces power transfer from the core.
Failing to rotate the fist to a vertical-then-horizontal position at extension, leaving the wrist misaligned and reducing structural integrity on impact.

movements.description: A direct punch targeting the centerline of the opponent, driving forward through the entire kinetic chain. A fundamental offensive technique shared across many kung fu styles.

movements.name: Zheng Ti Tui (Front Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Leaning the torso backward to compensate for limited hip flexor mobility, reducing power and exposing the centerline.
Extending the leg fully before the hip reaches full flexion, causing a pushing action rather than a snapping strike.

movements.description: A thrusting front kick that uses the heel or ball of the foot to drive powerfully into the target. Effective in both offensive and defensive roles due to its directness.

movements.name: Gou Tui (Hook Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Initiating the hooking arc from the knee rather than the hip, shortening the strike arc and losing the centrifugal momentum of the leg.
Pivoting the support foot insufficiently, which limits hip rotation and forces the body to compensate with lateral trunk lean.

movements.description: A kick that reaches past the target and hooks back to strike with the heel. The unusual hooking trajectory bypasses defenses that are angled squarely toward the attacker.

movements.name: Ma Bu (Horse Stance)

commonMistakes.description: Allowing the knees to cave inward (valgus collapse) rather than tracking over the toes, placing excessive stress on the medial knee ligaments.
Anteriorly tilting the pelvis and arching the lower back instead of maintaining a neutral spine with engaged abdominals.

movements.description: The foundational low wide stance that develops leg strength, stability, and a rooted connection to the ground. Training in horse stance is a fundamental rite of passage in most kung fu systems.

movements.name: Zhang Zhong Zhang (Palm Strike)

commonMistakes.description: Hyperextending the wrist backward at the moment of contact instead of maintaining a neutral wrist, which risks wrist sprain and diminishes force transmission.
Pushing the strike from the arm alone without engaging the pectorals and core rotation, producing a weak, arm-only strike with no whole-body power.

movements.description: An open-hand strike using the heel of the palm to transmit force without the fragility risks of a closed fist. Common in styles that emphasize internal power and structural alignment.

movements.name: Xuan Feng Tui (Spinning Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Initiating the spin with the arm rather than driving from the supporting hip, causing a slow and uncontrolled rotation.
Looking away from the target before contact, reducing accuracy and leaving the fighter blind.

movements.description: A 360-degree spinning kick that harnesses rotational momentum for a powerful circular strike. Requires precise coordination, balance, and timing for safe and effective execution.

movements.name: Di Tang (Low Sweep)

commonMistakes.description: Sweeping with a bent leg instead of extending fully, reducing reach and power.

movements.description: A ground-level sweeping technique targeting the opponent's legs and ankles to disrupt their foundation. Creates takedown opportunities and teaches awareness of the lower gate.

movements.name: Bao Quan (Leopard Punch)

commonMistakes.description: Using full fist contact instead of the second knuckle row, losing the concentrated impact of the technique.

movements.description: A compact punch using the second knuckles of a half-closed fist to concentrate force. The smaller striking surface targets pressure points and sensitive areas with greater precision.

movements.name: Gong Bu (Bow Stance)

commonMistakes.description: Positioning the front foot at an angle rather than pointing directly forward, misaligning the knee and hip and limiting power transfer.
Bending the back knee instead of fully extending it, losing the pushing leverage of the rear leg.

movements.description: A forward-weighted stance resembling a drawn bow, providing a stable base for powerful linear techniques. One of the five fundamental stances that underpin kung fu movement.

movements.name: Ce Ti Tui (Crescent Kick)

commonMistakes.description: Swinging the leg with a bent knee, converting the sweeping arc into a knee-dominant kick that loses the full leg extension and reach.
Allowing the torso to lean away from the arc to compensate for limited hip mobility, disrupting balance and reducing striking height.

movements.description: A sweeping kick that traces a wide arc across the body with an extended leg. Used to knock away guarding limbs or strike the side of the head with the sole or instep.

movements.name: Zhou Ji (Elbow Strike)

commonMistakes.description: Striking with only arm movement rather than driving from the hip rotation, removing the bodyweight from the strike.
Pulling the opposite hand back without purpose, wasting the counter-rotation that could amplify the striking side's power.

movements.description: A short-range devastating strike using the point of the elbow against close targets. Delivers tremendous force over a small contact area, highly effective at grappling distance.

movements.name: Shuang Chui (Double Punch)

commonMistakes.description: Throwing both punches simultaneously rather than in rapid sequence, eliminating the rotational power from each individual strike.
Dropping the guard completely to deliver the second punch, leaving the head unprotected during the transition.

movements.description: A simultaneous two-handed strike that overwhelms a single guard or attacks two targets at once. The coordinated bilateral motion trains symmetrical power generation and timing.
